Label: AJCCClinicalM
Description: Extent of the distant metastasis for the cancer based on evidence obtained from clinical assessment parameters determined prior to treatment.

Label: PrimaryGleasonGrade
Description: The text term used to describe the primary Gleason score, which describes the pattern of cells making up the largest area of the tumor. The primary and secondary Gleason pattern grades are combined to determine the patient's Gleason grade group, which is used to determine the aggresiveness of prostate cancer. Note that this grade describes the entire prostatectomy specimen and is not specific to the sample used for sequencing.

Label: MethodofDiagnosis
Description: Text term used to describe the method used to confirm the patients malignant diagnosis.
Valid Values: ['Not Reported', 'Bone Marrow Aspirate', 'unknown', 'Biopsy', 'Pap Smear', 'Cystoscopy', 'Core Biopsy', 'Cytology', 'Ultrasound Guided Biopsy', 'Debulking', 'Dilation and Curettage Procedure', 'Laparoscopy', 'Blood Draw', 'Incisional Biopsy', 'Autopsy', 'Fine Needle Aspiration', 'Diagnostic Imaging', 'Laparotomy', 'Pathologic Review', 'Enucleation', 'Thoracentesis', 'Surgical Resection', 'Other', 'Physical Exam', 'Excisional Biopsy', '']

Label: VascularInvasionType
Description: Text term that represents the type of vascular tumor invasion.

Label: BreslowThickness
Description: The number that describes the distance, in millimeters, between the upper layer of the epidermis and the deepest point of tumor penetration.

Label: HTANParticipantID
Description: HTAN ID associated with a patient based on HTAN ID SOP (eg HTANx_yyy )

Label: NonNodalTumorDeposits
Description: The yes/no/unknown indicator used to describe the presence of tumor deposits in the pericolic or perirectal fat or in adjacent mesentery away from the leading edge of the tumor.

Label: PerineuralInvasionPresent
Description: A yes/no indicator to ask if perineural invasion or infiltration of tumor or cancer is present.

Label: ClassificationofTumor
Description: Text that describes the kind of disease present in the tumor specimen as related to a specific timepoint.

Label: INPCGrade
Description: Text term used to describe the classification of neuroblastic differentiation within neuroblastoma tumors, as defined by the International Neuroblastoma Pathology Classification (INPC).

Label: OvarianSurfaceInvolvement
Description: The text term that describes whether the surface tissue (outer boundary) of the ovary shows evidence of involvement or presence of cancer.

Label: PregnantatDiagnosis
Description: The text term used to indicate whether the patient was pregnant at the time they were diagnosed.

Label: MedulloblastomaMolecularClassification
Description: The text term used to describe the classification of medulloblastoma tumors based on molecular features.

Label: LymphaticInvasionPresent
Description: A yes/no indicator to ask if small or thin-walled vessel invasion is present, indicating lymphatic involvement

Label: TumorFocality
Description: The text term used to describe whether the patient's disease originated in a single location or multiple locations.

Label: IRSStage
Description: The text term used to describe the classification of rhabdomyosarcoma tumors, as defined by the Intergroup Rhabdomyosarcoma Study (IRS).

Label: AJCCPathologicT
Description: Code of pathological T (primary tumor) to define the size or contiguous extension of the primary tumor (T), using staging criteria from the American Joint Committee on Cancer (AJCC).

Label: SecondaryGleasonGrade
Description: The text term used to describe the secondary Gleason score, which describes the pattern of cells making up the second largest area of the tumor. The primary and secondary Gleason pattern grades are combined to determine the patient's Gleason grade group, which is used to determine the aggresiveness of prostate cancer. Note that this grade describes the entire prostatectomy specimen and is not specific to the sample used for sequencing.

Label: LastKnownDiseaseStatus
Description: Text term that describes the last known state or condition of an individual's neoplasm.
Valid Values: ['Not Reported', 'Loco-regional recurrence/progression', 'unknown tumor status', 'Not Allowed To Collect', 'Biochemical evidence of disease without structural correlate', 'Not Applicable', 'Tumor free', 'Distant met recurrence/progression', 'With tumor']

Label: AJCCClinicalStage
Description: Stage group determined from clinical information on the tumor (T), regional node (N) and metastases (M) and by grouping cases with similar prognosis for cancer.
Valid Values: ['Stage IA1', 'Not Reported', 'Stage IA2', 'unknown', 'Stage IIIC', 'Stage IIC', 'Stage X', 'Stage IVC', 'Stage IB', 'Stage IB1', 'Stage I', 'Stage Tis', 'Stage IIIA', 'Stage IVB', 'Stage IC', 'Stage IA', 'Stage IIIC2', 'Stage IIIB', 'Stage IB2', 'Stage IIIC1', 'Stage II', 'Stage IV', 'Stage IIC1', 'Stage IS', 'Stage IVA', 'Stage 0', 'Stage 0is', 'Stage III', 'Stage 0a', 'Stage IIA2', 'Stage IIA1', 'Stage IIB', 'Stage IIA', '']

Label: AJCCPathologicN
Description: The codes that represent the stage of cancer based on the nodes present (N stage) according to criteria based on multiple editions of the AJCC's Cancer Staging Manual.

Label: AJCCPathologicStage
Description: The extent of a cancer, especially whether the disease has spread from the original site to other parts of the body based on AJCC staging criteria.
Valid Values: ['Stage IA1', 'Not Reported', 'Stage IA2', 'unknown', 'Stage IIIC', 'Stage IIC', 'Stage X', 'Stage IVC', 'Stage IB', 'Stage IB1', 'Stage I', 'Stage Tis', 'Stage IIIA', 'Stage IVB', 'Stage IC', 'Stage IA', 'Stage IIIC2', 'Stage IIIB', 'Stage IB2', 'Stage IIIC1', 'Stage II', 'Stage IV', 'Stage IIC1', 'Stage IS', 'Stage IVA', 'Stage 0', 'Stage 0is', 'Stage III', 'Stage 0a', 'Stage IIA2', 'Stage IIA1', 'Stage IIB', 'Stage IIA', '']

Label: INSSStage
Description: Text term used to describe the staging classification of neuroblastic tumors, as defined by the International Neuroblastoma Staging System (INSS).

Label: MitoticCount
Description: The number of mitoses identified under the microscope in tumors. The method of counting varies, according to the specific tumor examined. Usually, the mitotic count is determined based on the number of mitoses per high power field (40X) or 10 high power fields.

Label: MitosisKaryorrhexisIndex
Description: Text term that represents the component of the International Neuroblastoma Pathology Classification (INPC) for mitosis-karyorrhexis index (MKI).

Label: GleasonGradeGroup
Description: The text term used to describe the overall grouping of grades defined by the Gleason grading classification, which is used to determine the aggressiveness of prostate cancer. Note that this grade describes the entire prostatectomy specimen and is not specific to the sample used for sequencing.

Label: MicropapillaryFeatures
Description: The yes/no/unknown indicator used to describe whether micropapillary features were determined to be present.

Label: LymphNodesTested
Description: The number of lymph nodes tested to determine whether lymph nodes were involved with disease as determined by a pathologic examination.

Label: LymphNodesPositive
Description: The number of lymph nodes involved with disease as determined by pathologic examination.

Label: YearofDiagnosis
Description: Numeric value to represent the year of an individual's initial pathologic diagnosis of cancer.

Label: LymphNodeInvolvedSite
Description: The text term used to describe the anatomic site of lymph node involvement.

Label: AnaplasiaPresent
Description: Yes/no/unknown/Not Reported indicator used to describe whether anaplasia was present at the time of diagnosis.

Label: DaystoLastFollowup
Description: Time interval from the date of last follow up to the date of initial pathologic diagnosis, represented as a calculated number of days. If not applicable please enter 'Not Applicable'

Label: Morphology
Description: The third edition of the International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, published in 2000 used principally in tumor and cancer registries for coding the site (topography) and the histology (morphology) of neoplasms. The study of the structure of the cells and their arrangement to constitute tissues and, finally, the association among these to form organs. In pathology, the microscopic process of identifying normal and abnormal morphologic characteristics in tissues, by employing various cytochemical and immunocytochemical stains. A system of numbered categories for representation of data.

Label: PeritonealFluidCytologicalStatus
Description: The text term used to describe the malignant status of the peritoneal fluid determined by cytologic testing.

Label: FirstSymptomPriortoDiagnosis
Description: Text term used to describe the patient's first symptom experienced prior to diagnosis and thought to be related to the disease.
Valid Values: ['Not Reported', 'Headaches', 'Seizures', 'unknown', 'Visual Changes', 'Altered Mental Status', 'Motor or Movement Changes', 'Sensory Changes', '']

Label: IRSGroup
Description: Text term used to describe the classification of rhabdomyosarcoma tumors, as defined by the Intergroup Rhabdomyosarcoma Study (IRS).
Valid Values: ['Not Reported', 'Group I', 'unknown', 'Group II', 'Group IIIb', 'Group Ia', 'Group Ib', 'Group IIIa', 'Group III', 'Group IV', 'Group IIa', 'Group IIc', '']

Label: PriorMalignancy
Description: The yes/no/unknown indicator used to describe the patient's history of prior cancer diagnosis.

Label: AJCCClinicalT
Description: Extent of the primary cancer based on evidence obtained from clinical assessment parameters determined prior to treatment.

Label: PercentTumorInvasion
Description: The percentage of tumor cells spread locally in a malignant neoplasm through infiltration or destruction of adjacent tissue.

Label: TumorGrade
Description: Numeric value to express the degree of abnormality of cancer cells, a measure of differentiation and aggressiveness.

Label: PriorTreatment
Description: A yes/no/unknown/not applicable indicator related to the administration of therapeutic agents received before the body specimen was collected.

Label: INRGStage
Description: The text term used to describe the staging classification of neuroblastic tumors, as defined by the International Neuroblastoma Risk Group (INRG).

Label: AJCCClinicalN
Description: Extent of the regional lymph node involvement for the cancer based on evidence obtained from clinical assessment parameters determined prior to treatment.

Label: MetastasisatDiagnosis
Description: The text term used to describe the extent of metastatic disease present at diagnosis.

Label: InternationalPrognosticIndex
Description: The text term used to describe the International Prognostic Index, which classifies the prognosis of patients with aggressive non-Hodgkin's lymphoma.

Label: INPCHistologicGroup
Description: The text term used to describe the classification of neuroblastomas distinguishing between favorable and unfavorable histologic groups. The histologic score, defined by the International Neuroblastoma Pathology Classification (INPC), is based on age, mitosis-karyorrhexis index (MKI), stromal content and degree of tumor cell differentiation.

Label: GleasonGradeTertiary
Description: The text term used to describe the tertiary pattern as described by the Gleason Grading System.

Label: VascularInvasionPresent
Description: The yes/no indicator to ask if large vessel or venous invasion was detected by surgery or presence in a tumor specimen.
Valid Values: ['Not Reported', 'unknown', 'no', 'Yes - Vascular Invasion Present', 'Not Allowed To Collect', '']

Label: CogNeuroblastomaRiskGroup
Description: Text term that represents the categorization of patients on the basis of prognostic factors per a system developed by Children's Oncology Group (COG). Risk level is used to assign treatment intensity.

Label: ProgressionorRecurrenceType
Description: The text term used to describe the type of progressive or recurrent disease or relapsed disease.
Conditional Requirements: ['Progression or Recurrence is "Yes - Progression or Recurrence"']

Label: DaystoProgressionFree
Description: Number of days between the date used for index and the date the patient's disease was formally confirmed as progression-free. If not applicable please enter 'Not Applicable'

Label: DaystoProgression
Description: Number of days between the date used for index and the date the patient's disease progressed. If not applicable please enter 'Not Applicable'

Label: DaystoRecurrence
Description: Number of days between the date used for index and the date the patient's disease recurred. If not applicable please enter 'Not Applicable'
Conditional Requirements: ['Progression or Recurrence is "Yes - Progression or Recurrence"']

Label: TreatmentIntentType
Description: Text term to identify the reason for the administration of a treatment regimen. [Manually-curated]

Label: TreatmentorTherapy
Description: A yes/no/unknown/not applicable indicator related to the administration of therapeutic agents received.

Label: TreatmentDose
Description: The numeric value used to describe the dose of an agent the patient received.

Label: TreatmentFrequency
Description: The text term used to describe the frequency the patient received an agent or regimen.
Valid Values: ['Not Reported', 'Every Other Day', 'Once Weekly', 'unknown', 'Five Times Daily', 'Three Times Daily', 'Twice Weekly', 'Four Times Daily', 'Every Hour', 'Every 24 Hours', 'Twice Daily', '']

Label: HTANParticipantID
Description: HTAN ID associated with a patient based on HTAN ID SOP (eg HTANx_yyy )

Label: Component
Description: Category of metadata (e.g. Diagnosis, Biospecimen, scRNA-seq Level 1, etc.); provide the same one for all items/rows.

Label: ReasonTreatmentEnded
Description: The text term used to describe the reason a specific treatment or regimen ended.
Valid Values: ['Other', 'Death', 'Course of Therapy Completed', 'Withdrawal by Subject', 'Disease Progression', 'Adverse Therapy Event', '']

Label: TreatmentOutcome
Description: Text term that describes the patient's final outcome after the treatment was administered.
Valid Values: ['Not Reported', 'Mixed Response', 'Treatment Stopped Due to Toxicity', 'unknown', 'No Response', 'Progressive Disease', 'Complete Response', 'Stable Disease', 'Very Good Partial Response', 'No Measurable Disease', 'Persistent Disease', 'Partial Response', 'Treatment Ongoing', '']

Label: TestAnalyteType
Description: The text term used to describe the type of analyte used for molecular testing. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.

Label: GeneSymbol
Description: The text term used to describe a gene targeted or included in molecular analysis. For rearrangements, this is should be used to represent the reference gene. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.

Label: Transcript
Description: Alphanumeric value used to describe the transcript of a specific genetic variant. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.

Label: VariantOrigin
Description: The text term used to describe the biological origin of a specific genetic variant. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.

Label: BloodTestNormalRangeLower
Description: Numeric value used to describe the lower limit of the normal range used to describe a healthy individual at the institution where the test was completed.

Label: Zygosity
Description: The text term used to describe the zygosity of a specific genetic variant.

Label: BloodTestNormalRangeUpper
Description: Numeric value used to describe the upper limit of the normal range used to describe a healthy individual at the institution where the test was completed.

Label: ClinicalBiospecimenType
Description: The text term used to describe the biological material used for testing, diagnostic, treatment or research purposes. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.
Valid Values: ['Not Reported', 'unknown', 'Serum', 'Connective Tissue', 'Peritoneal Fluid', 'Plasma', 'Cerebrospinal Fluid', 'Nerve Tissue', 'Buffy Coat', 'Embryonic Fluid', 'Uninvolved Tissue NOS', 'Bone Marrow', 'Urine', 'Muscle Tissue', 'Feces', 'Blood', 'Tissue NOS', 'Skin', 'Embryonic Tissue', 'Pleural Fluid', 'Granulocyte', 'Soft Tissue', 'Saliva', 'Buccal Mucosa', '']

Label: TestResult
Description: The text term used to describe the result of the molecular test. If the test result was a numeric value see test_value. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.
Valid Values: ['Not Reported', 'Intermediate', 'unknown', 'High', 'negative', 'Not Applicable', 'positive', 'Overexpressed', 'Loss of Expression', 'Abnormal NOS', 'Test Value Reported', 'Low', 'Copy Number Reported', 'Normal', 'Equivocal']

Label: StartDaysfromIndex
Description: Number of days from the date of birth (index date) to the date of an event (e.g. exposure to environmental factor, treatment start, etc.). If not applicable please enter 'Not Applicable'

Label: LociCount
Description: Numeric value used to describe the number of loci tested.

Label: SecondExon
Description: The second exon number involved in molecular variation. If a specific genetic variant is being reported, this property can be used to capture the second exon where that variant is located. This property is typically used for a translocation where two different locations are involved in the variation.

Label: MolecularConsequence
Description: The text term used to describe the molecular consequence of genetic variation. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.
Valid Values: ['Regulatory Region Ablation', 'Intergenic Variant', 'Non-coding Transcript Exon Variant', 'Mature miRNA Variant', 'Missense Variant', 'Transcript Ablation', 'Protein Altering Variant', 'Regulatory Region Amplification', 'Splice Donor Variant', 'Frameshift Variant', 'Regulatory Region Variant', 'Downstream Gene Variant', 'Coding Sequence Variant', 'Stop Lost', '3 Prime UTR Variant', 'Stop Retained Variant', 'TFBS Amplification', 'Inframe Insertion', 'Intron Variant', 'Start Lost', 'Transcript Amplification', 'TF Binding Site Variant', 'Incomplete Terminal Codon Variant', 'Splice Acceptor Variant', 'Synonymous Variant', '5 Prime UTR Variant', 'Non-coding Transcript Variant', 'Feature Truncation', 'Splice Region Variant', 'Stop Gain', 'TFBS Ablation', 'NMD Transcript Variant', 'Feature Elongation', 'Inframe Deletion', 'Upstream Gene Variant', '']

Label: AAChange
Description: Alphanumeric value used to describe the amino acid change for a specific genetic variant. Example: R116Q. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.

Label: StopDaysfromIndex
Description: Number of days from the date of birth (index date) to the end date of the event (e.g. exposure to environmental factor, treatment start, etc.). Note: if the event occurs at a single time point, e.g. a diagnosis or a lab test, the values for this column is 'Not Applicable'

Label: Clonality
Description: The text term used to describe whether a genomic variant is related by descent from a single progenitor cell. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.

Label: Exon
Description: Exon number targeted or included in a molecular analysis. If a specific genetic variant is being reported, this property can be used to capture the exon where that variant is located. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.

Label: TestValue
Description: The text term or numeric value used to describe a specific result of a molecular test. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here

Label: VariantType
Description: The text term used to describe the type of genetic variation.

Label: Chromosome
Description: The text term used to describe a chromosome targeted or included in molecular testing. If a specific genetic variant is being reported, this property can be used to capture the chromosome where that variant is located. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.

Label: TimepointLabel
Description: Label to identify the time point at which the clinical data or biospecimen was obtained (e.g. Baseline, End of Treatment, Overall survival, Final). NO PHI/PII INFORMATION IS ALLOWED.

Label: Pathogenicity
Description: The text used to describe a variant's level of involvement in the cause of the patient's disease according to the standards outlined by the American College of Medical Genetics and Genomics (ACMG).

Label: LaboratoryTest

Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.

Label: HistoneVariant
Description: The text term used to describe a specific histone variants, which are proteins that substitute for the core canonical histones.

Label: HistoneFamily
Description: The text term used to describe the family, or classification of a group of basic proteins found in chromatin, called histones.

Label: MolecularAnalysisMethod
Description: The text term used to describe the method used for molecular analysis. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.

Label: Intron
Description: Intron number targeted or included in molecular analysis. If a specific genetic variant is being reported, this property can be used to capture the intron where that variant is located.

Label: Cytoband
Description: Alphanumeric value used to describe the cytoband or chromosomal location targeted or included in molecular analysis. If a specific genetic variant is being reported, this property can be used to capture the cytoband where the variant is located. Format: [chromosome][chromosome arm].[band+sub-bands]. Example: 17p13.1. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.

Label: TestUnits
Description: The text term used to describe the units of the test value for a molecular test. This property is used in conjunction with test_value. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.

Label: CopyNumber
Description: Numeric value used to describe the number of times a section of the genome is repeated or copied within an insertion, duplication or deletion variant. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.

Label: SecondGeneSymbol
Description: The text term used to describe a secondary gene targeted or included in molecular analysis. For rearrangements, this is should represent the location of the variant. Note: This node is meant to capture molecular tests that were completed clinically for the participant and only includes data from diagnostic array that was completed prior to research sequencing was done. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.

Label: HTANParticipantID
Description: HTAN ID associated with a patient based on HTAN ID SOP (eg HTANx_yyy )

Label: Ploidy
Description: Text term used to describe the number of sets of homologous chromosomes.

Label: Component
Description: Category of metadata (e.g. Diagnosis, Biospecimen, scRNA-seq Level 1, etc.); provide the same one for all items/rows.

Label: LociAbnormalCount
Description: Numeric value used to describe the number of loci determined to be abnormal.

Label: Antigen
Description: The text term used to describe an antigen included in molecular testing. Do not include data related to research assay outputs here.

Label: CellCount
Description: Numeric value used to describe the number of cells used for molecular testing. Label: TopographyCode

Label: AcquisitionMethodType
Description: Records the method of acquisition or source for the specimen under consideration.
Valid Values: ['Blood draw', 'Biopsy', 'Not specified', 'Re-excision', 'Shave Biopsy', 'Sentinel Node Biopsy', 'Induced sputum', 'Endoscopic biopsy', 'Punch Biopsy', 'Autopsy', 'Fine Needle Aspirate', 'Fluid collection', 'Non induced sputum', 'Forceps biopsy', 'Cytobrush', 'Lymphadenectomy - Regional Nodes', 'Surgical Resection', 'Other Acquisition Method', 'BAL (bronchial alveolar lavage)', 'Core needle biopsy', 'Excision']

Label: HTANBiospecimenID

Label: FixativeType

Valid Values: ['NP40 lysis buffer', 'PAXgene tissue', 'unknown', 'RNAlater', '95% Ethanol', 'Dimidoester', 'Acetone', 'Carbodiimide', 'TCL lysis buffer', 'Alcohol', 'Dimethylacetamide', 'Cryo-store', 'Unfixed', 'Methacarn', 'Formalin', 'Saline', 'OCT media', "Carnoy's Fixative", 'Other', 'Para-benzoquinone', 'Glutaraldehyde', 'Polaxamer', 'None']

Label: TimepointLabel
Description: Label to identify the time point at which the clinical data or biospecimen was obtained (e.g. Baseline, End of Treatment, Overall survival, Final). NO PHI/PII INFORMATION IS ALLOWED.

Label: BiospecimenType

Label: HTANParentID
Description: HTAN ID of parent from which the biospecimen was obtained. Parent could be another biospecimen or a research participant.

Label: AdditionalTopography

Valid Values: ['Not Reported', 'skin of vulva', 'skin of breast', 'skin of eye lid', 'skin NOS', 'Hilar Airway', 'Peri-tumoral Airway', 'skin of scalp', 'skin of penis', 'skin of back', 'skin of abdomen', 'skin of other parts of face', 'skin of lip', 'skin of lower limb and hip', 'skin of palm', 'Skin of trunk', 'skin of nose', 'skin of upper limb and shoulder', 'skin of sole', 'skin of neck', 'skin of chest', 'skin of scrotum', 'skin of ear', '']

Label: SourceHTANBiospecimenID
Description: This is the HTAN ID that may have been assigned to the biospecimen at the site of biospecimen origin (e.g. BU).

Label: AdjacentBiospecimenIDs
Description: List of HTAN Identifiers (separated by commas) of adjacent biospecimens cut from the same sample; for example HTA3_3000_3, HTA3_3000_4, ...

Label: Component
Description: Category of metadata (e.g. Diagnosis, Biospecimen, scRNA-seq Level 1, etc.); provide the same one for all items/rows.

Label: ProtocolLink
Description: Protocols.io ID or DOI link to a free/open protocol resource describing in detail the assay protocol (e.g. surface markers used in Smart-seq, dissociation duration,  lot/batch numbers for key reagents such as primers, sequencing reagent kits, etc.) or the protocol by which the sample was obtained or generated.

Label: CollectionMedia
Description: Material Specimen is collected into post procedure

Label: AcquisitionMethodOtherSpecify
Description: A custom acquisition method [Text - max length 100 characters]

Description: The kind of molecular specimen analyte: a molecular derivative (I.e. RNA / DNA / Protein Lysate) obtained from a specimen

Label: SectionThicknessValue
Description: Numeric value to describe the thickness of a slice to tissue taken from a biospecimen, measured in microns (um).

Label: SlideChargeType
Description: A description of the charge on the glass slide.

Label: FixationDuration
Description: The length of time, from beginning to end, required to process or preserve biospecimens in fixative (measured in minutes)

Label: SectioningDaysfromIndex
Description: Number of days from the research participant's index date that the biospecimen was sectioned after collection. If not applicable please enter 'Not Applicable'
